description: The Stopwatch Gang was a group of three Canadian bank robbers active from 1974 to 1980. They robbed over 140 banks in the United States and Canada, stealing approximately $15 million Canadian dollars. Known for meticulous planning and non-violent methods, they completed robberies in under two minutes. The gang was named by the FBI after a member wore a stopwatch during a heist.

country_links.evidence_quote: they carried out a large number of bank robberies in the 1980s along the California coast... The gang's biggest heist took place at a Bank of America branch in San Diego
country_links.context: The Stopwatch Gang robbed banks in the United States, including a gold heist at Ottawa airport (Canada) and many bank robberies along the California coast, with their biggest heist in San Diego.
